page: Amazon Web Services c8ib.12xlarge is a compute-optimized eighth-generation instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 dedicated physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which minimizes virtualization overhead to deliver near-bare-metal performance.

The c8ib.12xlarge instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators. The lack of local storage and hardware acceleration represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance strictly for compute-bound workloads that rely on external storage systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8ib.12xlarge offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory footprint. This makes it highly efficient for workloads that require raw CPU processing power without the overhead of massive memory allocations. Recommended workloads for this profile include high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed analytics, ad serving, and compute-intensive web applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance designed for high-performance workloads. Powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz, it delivers 48 vCPUs across 24 dedicated physical cores, paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance ensures minimal virtualization overhead. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps but does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand large memory capacities or local disk I/O. The c8ib.12xlarge is suited for batch processing, high-performance computing, distributed analytics, and compute-intensive web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of memory, and a 3.9 GHz Intel processor, designed for high-performance computing and batch processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8ib.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual instance featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processors and high-speed networking for intensive processing workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 dedicated physical Intel cores operating at 3.9 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators, optimized for external storage
Suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, and distributed analytics
